data.title: Update project docs to reflect ticket archived-marker fast-skip and python pipeline consolidation
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the changes introduced by S1a, S1b, and S2. Targets existing docs only — do not create new documentation files or patterns.

**Why**: Future agents (reducers, reviewers, implementation planners) need accurate awareness of: (1) the `.archived` marker contract (how it is written, read, self-healed) so they do not bypass it; (2) the new `ticket_reducer/llm_format.py` module path so they don't follow the stale importlib pattern; (3) the new `ticket-archive-markers-backfill.sh` deploy step.

**Scope**:
- IN: Update `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md` (document `ticket-archive-markers-backfill.sh` under the compact/archive section); update `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` (describe the `.archived` marker, its write ordering contract, self-heal behavior, and the `compute_dir_hash()` extension; update the `ticket-llm-format.py` reference to point to `ticket_reducer/llm_format.py`); update `CLAUDE.md` Architecture section only if there is a reference to the subprocess-pipeline pattern or the ticket-llm-format.py module path that becomes stale
- OUT: Creating any new documentation file; creating new ADRs (epic changes are refinements to existing infrastructure, not new architectural decisions); updating KNOWN-ISSUES.md (no new known issues introduced)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md` documents the `ticket-archive-markers-backfill.sh` one-shot script (purpose, usage, when to run)
  ← Satisfies: implicit — documentation must reflect new command surface
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` describes the `.archived` marker contract (who writes, when, what the read path checks, self-heal on orphan) and references the new `ticket_reducer/llm_format.py` module path instead of the stale `ticket-llm-format.py` script
  ← Satisfies: implicit — architecture doc must reflect current state
- When this story is complete, `CLAUDE.md` is updated if any existing rule or quick-reference refers to the old subprocess-pipeline pattern or the `ticket-llm-format.py` module path (otherwise no change)
  ← Satisfies: implicit — CLAUDE.md Architecture section must not reference stale patterns

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Style: follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting and conventions. Concise updates only — no narrative expansion.
- [Maintainability] Depends on S1a, S1b, and S2 — runs after all implementation stories.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
